content.body: Noam Rosenthal: can you review https://github.com/whatwg/infra/pull/713 perhaps?
content.formatted_body: Noam Rosenthal: can you review https://github.com/whatwg/infra/pull/713 perhaps?

content.body: Jake Archibald: did you look into the usage of "flat tree" in popover perhaps? I wonder if it makes sense given that display: contents or display: none takes an element out of the flat tree (as it's primarily a layout concept). Perhaps it works because popovers are in the top layer, but I wonder if implementations actually use the layout tree for this or something else.
content.formatted_body: Jake Archibald: did you look into the usage of "flat tree" in popover perhaps? content.body: jmdyck: I'm not sure if you have been working on it as well, but I've been putting a little bit of time trying to figure out the HTML/IDL side of MakeRealm with Shannon Booth.
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: I'm not sure if you have been working on it as well, but I've been putting a little bit of time trying to figure out the HTML/IDL side of MakeRealm with Shannon Booth.

content.body: I just noticed the update to webidl #1547
(and no, I haven't been working on it)
jmdyck: one thing I'm finding is that we might have to set realm.[[GlobalObject]] to make "is exposed" work correctly. At least, that would give us a fairly minimal change.
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: one thing I'm finding is that we might have to set realm.[[GlobalObject]] to make "is exposed" work correctly. At least, that would give us a fairly minimal change.

content.body: An alternative might be storing the global interface type on the environment. Maybe we should do that to keep the layering somewhat clean.
I suspect I would have to read a fair bit and draw some diagrams before I could make an intelligent response.
